title: 5th STM main group meeting: Member States and co-chairs discuss progress on implementing the EU approach on space traffic management
content: In its 5th meeting on 8 June 2026, the STM main group, co-chaired by DG DEFIS and EEAS, was debriefed on the latest activities of the STM subgroups followed up by a comprehensive overview of the work of STM subgroup 1 which has been compiling and aggregating civilian and military STM requirements ou

title: EU Commission and EU Drugs Agency present the 2026 European Drug Report
content: The report shows that drugs have an increasingly negative impact on European health and security as they are more available, diverse and potent. The report is based on data from 29 countries (27 EU Member States, Türkiye and Norway) and covers drug use and supply as well as drug-related harms.

title: The European Union and Australia successfully conclude Horizon Europe negotiations
content: Research and innovation news alert: Today, the European Commission and Australia have successfully concluded negotiations on Australia’s association to Horizon Europe, the the EU's flagship €93.5 billion research and innovation funding programme.

title: Commission updates EU Air Safety List: all air carriers from Kyrgyzstan removed, Air Express Algeria added
content: The Commission updated the EU Air Safety List, the list of airlines that are subject to an operating ban or operational restrictions within the European Union, because they do not meet international safety standards.

title: OceanEye initiative places EU at the forefront of ocean observation
content: The European Commission has expanded the EU's role in ocean observation by adopting OceanEye. This initiative aims to position the EU as the world's leading provider of ocean intelligence.
snippet: The European Commission has expanded the EU's role in ocean observation by adopting OceanEye. This initiative aims to position the EU as the world's leading provider of ocean intelligence.

title: Five things you should know about climate scenarios
content: Scientists constantly investigate the state of our planet and our greenhouse gas emissions and policy choices. This allows them to make projections about what our climate will look like in the future, based on different paths we might follow.

title: Technical Study on Indirect Emissions in the CBAM
content: The study provides policy analysis to support the implementation and potential evolution of the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM).
snippet: The study provides policy analysis to support the implementation and potential evolution of the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM).

title: Call for tender published for the initial EU space weather service
content: A call for tender for procuring the initial EU space weather service under the Space Weather Events (SWE) subcomponent of the EU Space Programme has been published.
snippet: A call for tender for procuring the initial EU space weather service under the Space Weather Events (SWE) subcomponent of the EU Space Programme has been published.

title: EU Ministers back cross-border initiative for autonomous vehicle testbeds

title: EU National Anti-Trafficking Coordinators and Rapporteurs discuss preparedness, crisis response and national action plans to fight trafficking in human beings
content: The meeting focused on preparedness and crisis response mechanisms for trafficking in human beings as well as the development and implementation of national action plans to support the fight against trafficking.

title: One year of the European Ocean Pact: leading global ocean protection and boosting the blue economy

title: EBRD and EU step up support for digital and green investments

title: Commission approves €100 million Austrian State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity
content: The European Commission has approved a €100 million Austrian scheme to support clean technology ('cleantech') manufacturing capacity, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This measure will contribute to the transition towards a net-zero economy.

title: June infringements package: key decisions on energy
content: In its regular package of infringement decisions, the European Commission pursues legal action against Member States for failing to comply with their obligations under EU law.

title: President von der Leyen at the EU-Western Balkans Summit in Montenegro to reaffirm EU's commitment to the region*

title: New EMN inform examines alternatives to providing housing in-kind for applicants for international protection

title: EU-Moldova Investment Conference announces up to €641 million in strategic investments

content: Today I spoke with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan about Russia's recent restrictions targeting Armenia.
snippet: Today I spoke with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan about Russia's recent restrictions targeting Armenia.

title: Shaping Europe’s startup future: Commissioner Zaharieva meets young innovators in Sofia

title: EU to mobilise up to €25 billion for renewable energy and clean technology by 2035 through Pact for the Mediterranean initiative

title: Two new members join the Alliance on Torture-Free Trade
content: On 9 June 2026, Andorra and Monaco joined the Alliance on Torture-Free Trade (Global Alliance).

title: Commission hosts 20th edition of the European Sustainable Energy Week
content: Taking place both onsite and online, this year's anniversary edition has the theme ‘A clean, secure and competitive Energy Union'.
snippet: Taking place both onsite and online, this year's anniversary edition has the theme ‘A clean, secure and competitive Energy Union'.

title: Commission imposes interim measures on Meta to preserve free access to WhatsApp for rival AI assistants
content: The European Commission has ordered Meta to restore free access to WhatsApp for rival general-purpose AI assistants, and maintain it until the end of the Commission's antitrust investigation.
snippet: The European Commission has ordered Meta to restore free access to WhatsApp for rival general-purpose AI assistants, and maintain it until the end of the Commission's antitrust investigation.

title: Commission approves €23 billion Italian State aid scheme to support renewable electricity production
content: The European Commission has approved a €23 billion Italian State aid scheme to support electricity production from renewable energy sources, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €23 billion Italian State aid scheme to support electricity production from renewable energy sources, in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal.

title: EU exports of shark fins dropped in 2025
content: In 2025, the EU exported 2.7 thousand tonnes of shark fins to countries outside the EU, worth €44.8 million. Compared with 2024, the export volume decreased by 15.2% (from 3.1 thousand tonnes), and the export value dropped by 31.3% (from €65.3 million).

title: EU implements Palestine facility with $395 million for local banks and €2.1 million in technical assistance

title: EU disburses nearly €2.8 billion to support Ukraine's financial stability and reform efforts
content: The European Commission has released nearly €2.8 billion to Ukraine today to support its financing needs and maintain public administration, as it continues to defend itself against Russia's war of aggression.

title: Commissioner Kos visits Ukraine to support reforms towards EU accession
content: Today, Commissioner Kos is in Kyiv. Her visit will focus on supporting Ukraine's EU accession reforms, wartime recovery, and the strengthening of political and societal support for European integration.

title: Justice and Home Affairs Council
content: On 4 June, Justice and Home Affairs Ministers from EU Member States and Schengen Associated Countries met at the JHA Council in Luxembourg. Among other topics, Commissioner for Internal Affairs and Migration Magnus Brunner presented the main findings of the 5th Report on the state of Schengen.

title: Guidance and legal text on temporary flat fee on low-value imports which will apply until 1 July 2028
content: From 1 July 2026, the EU will apply a temporary €3 customs duty per item on low-value consignments (up to €150) imported from outside the EU, abolishing the duty exemption applicable until 30 June 2026.

title: Armenia: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the EU on the outcome of parliamentary elections
content: The European Union congratulates the citizens of the Republic of Armenia on exercising their sovereign democratic right to vote in parliamentary elections and commends the high level of civic participation.

title: “From lab marvel to global guardian": Bacterial cellulose could help tackle hazardous substances in Europe’s waters
content: Bacterial cellulose, a biodegradable material produced by bacteria, could have “transformative potential” in remediating water contaminated by hazardous substances, according to a new review.
snippet: Bacterial cellulose, a biodegradable material produced by bacteria, could have “transformative potential” in remediating water contaminated by hazardous substances, according to a new review.

title: Seafloor marine heatwaves are becoming more frequent, but are rarely captured by current models
content: Seafloor marine heatwaves are often overlooked despite risks to sensitive ecosystems. Modelling of the northwestern European ocean shelf suggests that under climate change heatwaves will increase faster on the seabed than on the surface.
snippet: Seafloor marine heatwaves are often overlooked despite risks to sensitive ecosystems. Modelling of the northwestern European ocean shelf suggests that under climate change heatwaves will increase

title: Reindeer grazing can help maintain tundra ecosystems, counteracting treeline advance due to climate change
content: Climate change is driving treelines upslope and northwards. A Nordic study finds that heavy reindeer grazing prevents birch forest expansion into mountain tundra, protected under the EU Habitats Directive.
snippet: Climate change is driving treelines upslope and northwards. A Nordic study finds that heavy reindeer grazing prevents birch forest expansion into mountain tundra, protected under the EU Habitats

title: EDF’s first-ever FSTP sub-call attracts strong interest from newcomers to EU defence funding
content: The submission phase of the first-ever Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) sub-call under the European Defence Fund (EDF)-funded MAJoR project has successfully concluded, attracting strong interest from innovative small and medium-sized companies across Europe.
